---
title: "Session 06 teaching notes: Python 3"
session_id: session-06
event_id: null
---

## Session 6 — Statistics, charts, and grouping

ggf. Varianz/Mittelwert erkären

Plots: nur LIne/Bar in der Klausur (siehe Dokument: relevant topics for the exam)

-> ML/prediction: nicht Klausurrelevant


Focus on descriptive statistics, basic charts, filtering, and grouping; distinguish exam-relevant content from enrichment.

## Exercises

| Page(s) | Exercise                 | Summary                                                                                                      | Files                                                                     | In-class / take-home |
| ------: | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
|      42 | Descriptive statistics   | Calculate aggregates, summaries, correlations, distinct values, and continent frequencies.                   | `countries.csv`<br>`countries3.py`                                        |                      |
|      49 | Basic visualizations     | Create line, bar, pie, density, and box plots for the country data.                                          | `countries.csv`<br>`countries4.py`                                        |                      |
|      53 | Conditional filtering    | Filter countries using area, population, continent, and GDP conditions; summarize and visualize the results. | `countries.csv`<br>`countries5.py`                                        |                      |
|      56 | Grouping and aggregation | Use `groupby()` to summarize countries by continent and insurance contracts by state and coverage.           | `countries.csv`<br>`countries6.py`<br>`insurance.xlsx`<br>`insurance1.py` |                      |

## Page 42 — Descriptive statistics

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/data/countries.csv`](../materials/data/countries.csv){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_06/countries3.py`](../materials/session_06/countries3.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_06/countries3.py >}}
```

- Explain variance and the mean if students need a refresher.

## Page 49 — Basic visualizations

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/data/countries.csv`](../materials/data/countries.csv){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_06/countries4.py`](../materials/session_06/countries4.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_06/countries4.py >}}
```

::: {.callout-important title="Exam relevance"}
Only line and bar plots are relevant to the exam; see the document listing exam-relevant topics.
:::

::: {.teaching-break}
☕ Break — 10 minutes
:::

## Page 53 — Conditional filtering

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/data/countries.csv`](../materials/data/countries.csv){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_06/countries5.py`](../materials/session_06/countries5.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_06/countries5.py >}}
```

- Connect filtering conditions to the resulting summaries and visualizations.

## Page 56 — Grouping and aggregation

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/data/countries.csv`](../materials/data/countries.csv){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_06/countries6.py`](../materials/session_06/countries6.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_06/countries6.py >}}
```

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/data/insurance.xlsx`](../materials/data/insurance.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_06/insurance1.py`](../materials/session_06/insurance1.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_06/insurance1.py >}}
```

- Demonstrate grouping with both the country and insurance datasets.

::: {.callout-note title="Exam relevance"}
Machine learning and prediction are not relevant to the exam.
:::

## Summary and announcements

- TODO

::: {.callout-tip title="Notes for improvement"}
Take notes on improvements and common questions during the session and add them to `feedback.qmd` afterwards.
:::
